title: Uganda's clinical trial landscape: 783 registered trials for 48 million people
primary_estimand: Condition-specific trial counts and sponsor classification for Uganda interventional trials
certainty: high (complete registry enumeration with trial-level data)
body: In Uganda, does the registered clinical trial portfolio align with the country's disease burden and population health needs? We retrieved all 783 interventional trials listing a Ugandan site on ClinicalTrials.gov and extracted sponsor, phase, condition, enrollment, site count, and current status for each record, classifying sponsors as Uganda-local or foreign. HIV accounted for 329 trials, or 42% of the total portfolio, while diabetes had only 3 trials, stroke had 6, and hypertension had 10, revealing extreme disease concentration. Foreign institutions sponsored 79.5% of all trials, and Makerere University alone accounted for 71% of all locally sponsored research output. Phase 1 local sovereignty was only 2.1%, with a single locally led phase 1 study identified among 48 total phase 1 trials. The portfolio therefore appears structurally dependent on foreign sponsors and concentrated in donor-driven infectious-disease priorities rather than reflecting local burden. The analysis is limited to ClinicalTrials.gov and cannot capture trials registered elsewhere or unregistered community-based studies.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used in early drafting. The final text was rewritten by the author, who takes full responsibility for the content.

checks.name: primary estimate in sentence 4
checks.detail: Sentence 4 reports HIV 329/42%, diabetes 3, stroke 6, HTN 10.
